title: Aggregation, Grouping, HAVING, and Honest Metrics · Relational Workshop
summary: A service lead needs ticket counts and response effort by team without double counting or hiding teams with no work. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of an audited service-metrics query pack while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX while predicting empty-set, NULL, and type behavior.
Set result grain with GROUP BY and verify that every selected value is grouped or aggregated.
Separate row filtering in WHERE from group filtering in HAVING.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Aggregate Functions
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 5 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Metric Validation
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-metric-validation-x8o0vm" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; aggregate grain, denominator, or reconciliation evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Metric Validation.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Metric Validation into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Metric Validation, keep Metric Validation connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-metric-validation-x8o0vm
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT team_id, COUNT(*) AS unresolved_count
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved'
GROUP BY team_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1
ORDER BY team_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
